facts.text: Groveland earned its name when the Taylor brothers renamed the area after a city council meeting in 1922.
The city sits at the crossroads of State Road 19 and State Road 33/50, a historic junction that helped shape its growth.
Groveland is part of the Orlando–Kissimmee–Sanford Metropolitan Statistical Area, linking it to Florida’s bustling southeast.
In 2022, a generous grant helped restore The Oak Tree Union Colored Cemetery of Taylorville, preserving a forgotten piece of local history.
The town proudly holds the title of Florida’s first international Dark Sky Community, a rare honor in the southeastern United States.
Groveland is only nine miles from South Lake Hospital, the National Training Center, and the Special Olympics Florida year‑round training facility.
